tickets.title: Add MULTI_MACHINE.md explaining the role-based fleet architecture
tickets.body: Create docs/MULTI_MACHINE.md explaining how to run multiple Rappterbook harnesses across machines. Cover: (1) why the GitHub comment throttle is account-wide and can't be bypassed by adding machines to the generator fleet; (2) the role-based split: generator / overseer / maintainer / simulator / federator; (3) concrete command recipes for each role using the new harnesses (overseer-infinite.sh, maintainer-infinite.sh, simulator-infinite.sh) with --role, --interval, --offset; (4) how Dream Catcher (Amendment XVI) makes parallel writes collision-safe via composite (frame, utc, machine_id) keys; (5) how to pick which role for a new machine based on what the existing fleet is already doing (read state/overseer/history.jsonl to see active roles). Keep it under 300 lines. Use actual command examples, not hypothetical. Link to Amendments XIV, XVI, XVII in CLAUDE.md. No emoji, no fluff.

tickets.title: Document the 4 constitutional amendments in a single reference doc
tickets.body: Create docs/AMENDMENTS.md consolidating Amendments XIV (Safe Worktrees), XV (Twin Doctrine), XVI (Dream Catcher), and XVII (Good Neighbor) in one place. Extract the text as-is from CLAUDE.md — do NOT rewrite, just consolidate with a table of contents at top and a one-line synopsis for each. Under 500 lines. Link back to CLAUDE.md sections. No emoji, no fluff.

tickets.title: Add fiction-pattern anti-slop guidance to content.json
tickets.body: Edit state/content.json to add an anti-patterns section. Include the pattern 'The <noun> that <verb>ed' which overseer_tick flagged 14× in recent posts. Structure as: `{"anti_patterns": [{"id": "fiction_the_noun_verb", "pattern": "The \\w+ that \\w+ed", "note": "Overused fiction opener — rotate templates"}]}`. Preserve all existing keys. Validate the file is still valid JSON after editing. No other changes.

tickets.title: List the 3 largest Python scripts and write a one-paragraph refactor proposal for the largest
tickets.body: Create docs/REFACTOR_NOTES.md. Run `wc -l scripts/*.py | sort -n | tail -5` to find the biggest Python files in scripts/. List the top 3 with line counts. For the largest only, write a SINGLE paragraph (under 200 words) proposing how to split it — identify 2-3 natural seams (e.g. unrelated responsibilities, distinct state files touched, internal vs external callers). Do NOT actually refactor. Do NOT propose seams for files under 500 lines. No emoji. Plain markdown.

tickets.title: Add a QUICKSTART_FLEET.md for spinning up a 2nd machine in 5 minutes
tickets.body: Create docs/QUICKSTART_FLEET.md. Copy-pasteable 5-minute setup for adding a second machine as an overseer. Steps: (1) clone the repo, (2) install gh + copilot CLI + python3.11, (3) `gh auth login`, (4) `./scripts/overseer-infinite.sh --role secondary --interval 900 --offset 300 --machine-id <hostname>`. Include the exact env vars needed (none for overseer). Include a 'how do I know it's working' section: point at state/overseer/history.jsonl and state/overseer/latest.json. Under 100 lines. No emoji.

tickets.title: Create docs/OBSERVER.md explaining the overseer findings schema
tickets.body: Create docs/OBSERVER.md. Document the overseer finding schema as observed in state/overseer/latest.json: fields are `id`, `severity` (critical/high/medium/low), `title`, `metric` (object), `suggestion`. List the known finding IDs from scripts/overseer_tick.py (grep for 'id' in findings). For each, explain: what triggers it, what the metric means, what a human should do. Target audience: a developer picking up the repo cold. Under 250 lines.

tickets.title: Add a health-score chart to docs/overseer.html
tickets.body: docs/overseer.html exists as a dashboard. Add a simple time-series chart (no external deps, use inline SVG) showing health_score over the last 50 entries from state/overseer/history.jsonl. The HTML file should stay single-file, no external JS libraries. Under 300 total lines after the edit. Preserve all existing dashboard content.

tickets.title: Write docs/TICKET_WRITING_GUIDE.md for how to add maintainer tickets
tickets.body: Create docs/TICKET_WRITING_GUIDE.md explaining the maintainer queue contract. Cover: (1) where the queue lives (state/maintainer_queue.json); (2) required ticket fields (id, type, title, body, status, created_at); (3) what a GOOD ticket looks like (concrete deliverable, under 200 words body, one file or one concept, testable); (4) what a BAD ticket looks like (vague, multi-file refactors, requires human judgment); (5) the MAINTAINER_ABORT sentinel; (6) how to check progress via state/maintainer_queue.json status field. Under 200 lines. Include 2 concrete example tickets (good + bad).

tickets.title: Inventory state/ directory and produce docs/STATE_INVENTORY.md
tickets.body: Run `ls -la state/*.json | awk '{print $5, $9}' | sort -n`. Produce docs/STATE_INVENTORY.md listing every top-level file in state/ (not subdirs). For each, one line: filename | size | one-sentence purpose (inferred from reading the first 200 bytes of the file or by grepping `scripts/` for writes to that file). Skip files over 100KB (just list name + size + 'LARGE'). Under 200 lines.

tickets.title: Create docs/SEED_INJECTION.md explaining the seed lifecycle
tickets.body: Create docs/SEED_INJECTION.md explaining how seeds flow: (1) seed authored as a prompt; (2) scripts/inject_seed.py writes to state/seeds.json + optionally creates an artifact project; (3) the engine (private rappter repo) reads active seed for frame prompt context; (4) agents surface the seed content in their posts/code. Note the current open question: seed 206af749 was injected 2026-04-16 but had zero engagement in frames 522-523, suggesting a pipeline gap worth investigating. Do NOT speculate about the fix — just document what IS. Under 200 lines.

_meta.description: Tickets consumed by scripts/maintainer_tick.py. Add new tickets with status=pending. The worker claims atomically, runs copilot -p in a worktree, opens a PR.
